label: Moodles
title: Moodles
lead: Reference for moodle display, intensity tiers, and the player condition indicators mods commonly surface.
bodyHtml:
Status moodle bridge

CUCoreLib appends MoodleRegistry and queues custom entries and feeds them into the real vanilla MoodleManager during its normal update pass.

That means your custom moodle should behave like any other moodle. It'll needs a valid icon name, a display name, a description, an intensity that matches one of the background slots the game expects, and an important choice that decides whether it belongs in the main row or the side row.

When to add a moodle

Use a moodle when the player needs visible feedback about a custom status effect. Keep it lightweight. The normal pattern is to call MoodleRegistry.AddMoodle(...) each update while the condition is active; CUCoreLib keeps it visible briefly and avoids duplicate spam for the same warning.

MoodleRegistry.AddMoodle(
        1,
        someLoadedImageAsset,
        "Lead Poisoning",
        "You're feeling a bit woozy and fatigued...",
        critical: false,
        chippedOnly: false,
    );

AddMoodle parameters/fields

ParameterTypeWhat it does
intensityintPicks the vanilla moodle background tier. Use the intensity table below for the visual mapping.
iconSpriteSprite icon.
iconIdstringString-based overload only. Don't use, unless the icon already exists in MoodleManager.icons.
namestringMouseover title text shown on the moodle. Can be localized.
descriptionstringMouseover description text shown in the hover/details UI. Can be localized.
criticalboolAdds the vanilla critical overlay glow. This is separate from the intensity number.
chippedOnlyboolIf true, the moodle will only be displayed when the player has a chip.
importantboolIf false, the moodle will be displayed in the unimportant hidden-ish section to the right.
keystringOptional stable queue key. Supply this when a moodle changes severity over time. (Where you only want one moodle with the same key active at a time.)
holdSecondsfloatHow long the queued moodle stays visible without being refreshed. The default is 0.75f.

If you want animated icons instead, AddAnimatedMoodle(...) swaps the icon argument for animationId but otherwise follows the same parameter meanings.

The intensity value picks the same vanilla background frame used by the base game.

IntensityVisualNotes
0Lowest standard tier.
1Low warning tier.
2Moderate warning tier.
3Strong warning tier.
4Used by minor positive/sleep moodles.
5Higher positive or special tier.
6Strong positive or special tier.
7Special positive standard tier. (Life Support)
8Special last-tier background used by rare moodles. (Last Stand)
critical: trueCritical is an overlay behavior, not a separate intensity number
